source_metadata.title_raw: Ravinder S. Kapoor - I-20-017 - February 26, 2020 - Sacramento

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

February 26, 2020

Ravinder S. Kapoor
Senior Attorney
State Treasurer's Office
915 Capitol Mall, Suite 110
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. 1-20-017

Dear Mr. Kapoor:

This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of Fiona Ma, State Treasurer,
regarding the behested payment provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Because your
inquiry is general in nature, we are treating your request as one for informal assistance.²

Also note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1
FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is
not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us for
additional advice.

QUESTION

Given that Treasurer Fiona Ma is the Chair of the Scholarshare Investment Board ("SIB"), a
government entity that will fundraise for the California Kids Investment and Development Savings
Program Fund ("KIDS program"), a government program, must she disclose funds raised by SIB
for the KIDS program as "behested payments" under the Act?

CONCLUSION

Yes, funds raised for the KIDS program qualify as behested payments under the Act, given
that (1) the funds are given at the request or suggestion of Treasurer Ma and her agents, (2) full and
adequate consideration is not given in exchange for the funds, and (3) the funds are given
principally for a governmental purpose unrelated to Treasurer Ma's seeking or holding of elective
office. Unless the source of the funds is a local, state, or federal governmental agency, disclosure is

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal
written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

File No. I-20-017
Page No. 2

required any time a single source makes aggregate payments of $5,000 or more in the same
calendar year.

FACTS AS PRESENTED BY REQUESTER

Created by statute in 1997, SIB is an instrumentality of the State of California that
administers the State's 529 college savings plan. The Treasurer of the State of California, the
Honorable Fiona Ma, serves as chair of the SIB's seven-member board, and the State Treasurer's
Office provides administrative support to SIB in areas such as information technology, legal,
external communications, human resources, finance/budget, and facilities.

In 2019, the Legislature created the KIDS program and then amended the program later the
same year. (Senate Bill 77, c.53, 2019 and Assembly Bill 114, c. 413 of 201, respectively.) The
KIDS program seeks to expand access to higher education through savings. The Legislature
directed SIB to operate the KIDS program, and to support the opening of college savings accounts
for California resident children born on or after July 1, 2020. The Legislature only provided a one-
time $25 million general fund appropriation for the program. This funding is estimated by program
staff to be exhausted in approximately one to two years after the start of the program. Absent any
future appropriations by the Legislature, the only apparent future funding mechanism for the
program is fundraising. Specifically, SIB is authorized by statute to "accept gifts, grants, awards,
matching contributions, interest income, and appropriations from individuals, businesses, state and
local governmental entities, and third-party sources for the program." (Education Code Section
69996.6(b)(1).) Further, SIB is directed to report to the Department of Finance and the Legislature
by January 1, 2022 information pertaining to the KIDS program first year of implementation
including a "description of the board's efforts and success in soliciting philanthropic or nonstate
money to support the program." (Education Code Section 69996.6(c)(5).) You state that "[i]n
accordance with current Commission guidance, the Treasurer will not be featured in any
fundraising efforts conducted by SIB staff."

The conclusion to be drawn from the KIDS program legislation is that the Legislature's
apparent intent is that SIB fundraise between $10-20 million per year to fund the KIDS program
after the initial $25 million appropriation is exhausted. Given the large amount of fundraising
required to sustain the KIDS program, an aggressive annual fundraising effort will be required by
SIB. This effort will primarily be driven by SIB staff. However, the large dollar amounts required
may require the involvement of the SIB Chair, Treasurer Fiona Ma, in raising funds.

ANALYSIS

Under Section 82004.5 of the Act, the term "besheated payment" includes a payment made at
the behest of an elected officer, or his or her agent, and "it is clear from the surrounding
circumstances that the payment was made for purposes unrelated to the officer's seeking or holding
of elective office." (Section 82004.5(c).) The phrase "made at the behest of" means "made under
the control or at the direction of, in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concurrently with, at the
request or suggestion of, or with the express, prior consent of." (Section 82041.3; Regulation
18215.3.)

File No. I-20-017
Page No. 3

Section 84224 imposes reporting requirements for behested payments that satisfy each of
the following requirements:

(1) The payment is made at the behest of an elected officer or member of the Public
Utilities Commission;
(2) The behesting elected officer or member of the Public Utilities Commission does
not provide full and adequate consideration in exchange for the payment;
(3) The payment is made principally for a legislative, governmental, or charitable
purpose; and
(4) If made principally for a legislative or governmental purpose, the payment is
made by a person other than a state, local, or federal governmental agency.

(Section 84224(b).)

A behested payment that meets the above description must be reported and filed with the
official's agency within 30 days following the date on which the payment or payments equal or
exceed $5,000 in the aggregate from the same source in the same calendar year in which they are
made. (See Section 84224(a).) The report must include: "name of payor; address of payor; amount
of the payment or payments; date or dates the payment or payments were made; the name and
address of the payee; a brief description of the goods or services provided or purchased, if any; and
a description of the specific purpose for which the payment or payments were made." (Ibid.)
Thereupon, any additional payments made by that same single source must also be reported within
30 days after the payment was made. (Ibid.) Within 30 days after receipt of any such report, state
agencies must forward a copy of the report to the FPPC. (Ibid.)

Regulation 18215.3(b) provides a safe harbor excluding certain payments from the
definition of "made at the behest of" and the reporting requirements in Section 84224. This
provision applies to payments in response to a fundraising solicitation from a charitable
organization, unless the solicitation "features" an elected officer.

You have indicated that because the KIDS program has only been provided a one-time $25
million general fund appropriation, "[t]he conclusion to be drawn from the KIDS program
legislation is that the Legislature's apparent intent is that SIB fundraise between $10-20 million per
year to fund the KIDS program after the initial $25 million appropriation is exhausted." Treasurer
Ma serves as the Chair of the seven member SIB. You anticipate that "an aggressive annual
fundraising effort will be required by SIB" and "the large dollar amounts required may require the
involvement of the SIB Chair, Treasurer Fiona Ma, in raising funds." The facts indicate that the SIB
will make the solicitation, accept the payments for the program, and that the SIB is a government
entity, not a charitable organization.

Based on the facts provided, and absent a statutory exception to the behested payment
reporting requirements, the funds raised by SIB at the behest of Treasurer Ma or her agents will
qualify as behested payments and must be reported by Treasurer Ma whenever a single source has
paid an aggregate $5,000 to the KIDS program within the same calendar year. Any subsequent
payments by the same single source must also be reported. Such payments would be made "at the
behest of" Treasurer Ma or her agents any time the payments are made in response to a request or
suggesting by Treasurer Ma and her agents. (Section 84224(b)(1); 82041.3, Regulation 18215.3(a).)

File No. I-20-017
Page No. 4

Treasurer Ma and the SIB staff presumably would not be providing "full and adequate
consideration in exchange for the payment[s]," which would be made principally for a
governmental purpose. (Section 84224(b)(2)-(3).) Since the payments would be made principally
for a governmental purpose, reporting aggregate payments of $5,000 or more would be required if
the payment is made by a person other than a state, local, or federal governmental agency. (Section
84224(b)(4).)

Nonetheless, Regulation 18215.3(b) addresses narrow circumstances in which beshested
payment reporting is not required, because "[a] payment is not 'made at the behest of' an elected
officer or a PUC member and is not subject to behested payment reporting if the payment is made
in response to a fundraising solicitation from a charitable organization requesting a payment unless
the solicitation features an elected officer or PUC member." In this case, the facts provided indicate
that the KIDS program will be seeking funding necessary to continue its operation for governmental
and charitable purposes. Under these facts, we find that the KIDS program is both a governmental
entity and charitable organization for purposes of Regulation 18215.3(b). Accordingly, payments
made in response to a solicitation are not reportable by Treasurer Ma if she is not featured in the
solicitation.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Dave Bainbridge
General Counsel

By: Kevin Cornwall
Counsel, Legal Division

KMC:aja

sections.analysis:
· Under Section 82004.5 of the Act, the term "behested payment" includes a payment made at 
the behest of an elected officer, or his or her agent, and "it is clear from the surrounding 
circumstances that the payment was made for purposes unrelated to the of(icer's seeking or holding 
of elective office." (Section 82004.5(c).) The phrase "made at the behest of' means "made under 
the control or at the direction of, in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert with, at the 
request or suggestion of, or with the express, prior consent of." (Section 82041.3; Regulation

Section 84224 imposes reporting requirements for behested payments that satisfy each of

(2) The behesting elected officer or member of the Public Utilities Commission does

purpose; and 
(4) If made principally for a legislative or governmental purpose, the payment is 
made by a person other than a state, local, or federal governmental agency. 
(Section 84224(b ).) 
A behested payment that meets the above description must be reported and filed with the

made. (See Section 84224(a).) The report must include: "name of payor; address of payor; amount

address of the payee; a brief description of the goods or services provided or purchased, if any; and

Thereafter, any additional payments made by that same single source must also be reported within

agencies must forward a copy of the report to the FPPC. (Ibid.) 
Regulation 18215.3(b) provides a safe harbor excluding certain payments from the

You have indicated that because the KIDS program has only been provided a one-time $25
